title: sorbus melanocarpa
intro: Sorbus melanocarpa, also known as the black rowan, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its berries, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The berries of the black rowan are rich in anthocyanins, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The berries can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
